recipe.name: Sticky Barbecued Pork Ribs with Jacket Potatoes and Chive Sour Cream
instructions: Place the pork ribs in a large pot or braising pan. Add half of the quartered onion, half the garlic cloves, half the fennel seeds, and 1 star anise to the pot.
Fill the pot with enough water to cover the ribs completely. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer gently for 3 hours until the meat is tender and nearly falling off the bone.
Meanwhile, preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Scrub the potatoes and prick them several times with a fork. Rub with butter and salt, then bake for 60-75 minutes until tender.
While the ribs braise, make the barbecue sauce: In a medium saucepan, combine the remaining onion (chopped), remaining garlic (minced), remaining fennel seeds, and remaining star anise.
Add the 2 whole red chili peppers (seeds and all), sugar, ketchup, dark soy sauce, and chipotle sauce to the pan. Stir well to combine.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat, stirring until all the sugar dissolves. Then increase heat and bring to a boil for 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat and transfer the entire mixture (including star anise and chilies) to a blender. Blend until smooth and thick. Set aside.
When ribs are done braising, remove from the liquid and pat dry. Cut the rack into individual ribs or 2-rib portions.
Increase oven temperature to 425°F (220°C). Brush the ribs generously with the barbecue sauce, coating all sides.
Place the glazed ribs on a baking sheet lined with foil. Roast for 20 minutes, basting with more sauce halfway through, until sticky and caramelized.
While ribs finish roasting, make the sour cream: In a bowl, whisk the heavy cream with salt and lemon juice until it thickens slightly (about 2-3 minutes).
Fold in the chopped chives. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Remove ribs from oven and let rest for 5 minutes. Split open the baked potatoes and fluff with a fork.
Serve the sticky barbecued ribs alongside the jacket potatoes topped with generous dollops of chive sour cream.
recipe.storage: Store leftover ribs in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Extra barbecue sauce can be refrigerated for up to 1 week. Reheat ribs in a 350°F oven for 15-20 minutes until warmed through. The chive sour cream is best made fresh but can be stored for 2 days in the refrigerator.
recipe.serving_suggestions: These finger-licking ribs are perfect for a lazy weekend meal. Serve with extra napkins! The dish pairs wonderfully with a cold local craft beer or porter. For additional sides, consider coleslaw, corn on the cob, or a simple green salad. The ribs are also fantastic for casual entertaining - let guests help themselves and enjoy the relaxed, hands-on dining experience. Any leftover meat can be shredded and used in sandwiches, tacos, or over rice.
